req.title: System-Admin deletes team
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to delete a team through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers UI-driven team deletion by a system-admin with mandatory field validation and audit trail. Five tests are designed: (1) EP positive – happy-path deletion with audit check; (2) NEG – unauthenticated access returns 401; (3) NEG/DT – forbidden role returns 403; (4) BVA – team-name at max-length boundary is accepted; (5) STC – state transition from existing→deleted is verified. The violation in test[4] was kind=negative paired with heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) is a structural/positive heuristic tracing valid state changes; the fix is to change kind to 'positive' since the test verifies a valid state transition (active→deleted) and the second-deletion rejection is a natural system-consistency check under the same STC coverage, not a purely negative equivalence class. All other tests are preserved unchanged.

tests.name: test_admin_deletes_team_successfully
preconditions: A team with known id exists
actions: Click the delete button for the team
Query the audit log for the deletion record
expected: Team is removed from the /teams list
Response status is 200 or 201 with entity id
Audit record exists for the deleted team id
trace: R-WEB-011: audit record written on success
R-WEB-011: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_deletes_team_successfully(admin_page, create_team, audit_log_client):
    team = create_team(name="QA-Team-EP")
    team_id = team["id"]

    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/teams")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-testid='team-row-{team_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()

    row.locator("[data-testid='btn-delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete-btn']").click()

    expect(page.locator(f"[data-testid='team-row-{team_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    toast = page.locator("[data-testid='toast-success']")
    expect(toast).to_contain_text(str(team_id))

    audit = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=team_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist after deletion"
    assert audit["entity_id"] == team_id

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_team
preconditions: A team with known id exists
actions: Attempt DELETE request to /teams/{id} without auth token
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_team(base_url, create_team, audit_log_client):
    team = create_team(name="QA-Team-NEG-401")
    team_id = team["id"]

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}",
        headers={},
        timeout=10
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 but got {response.status_code}"
    )

    audit = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=team_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should exist for failed unauthenticated request"

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_cannot_delete_team
preconditions: User is authenticated but role is 'viewer' (not admin)
A team with known id exists
actions: Attempt DELETE request to /teams/{id} with viewer token
trace: R-WEB-011: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_non_admin_role_cannot_delete_team(base_url, viewer_auth_token, create_team, audit_log_client):
    team = create_team(name="QA-Team-NEG-403")
    team_id = team["id"]

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {viewer_auth_token}"},
        timeout=10
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 but got {response.status_code}"
    )

    audit = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=team_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written for forbidden deletion attempt"

tests.name: test_delete_team_with_max_length_name
preconditions: A team exists whose name is exactly MAX_TEAM_NAME_LEN characters
actions: Create a team with a name of exactly MAX_TEAM_NAME_LEN characters
Locate the team row by id
expected: Team with max-length name is deleted successfully
Audit record written for the entity
trace: R-WEB-011: boundary – max-length team name still processed by mandatory field validation
R-WEB-011: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_TEAM_NAME_LEN = 100

def test_delete_team_with_max_length_name(admin_page, create_team, audit_log_client):
    max_name = "T" * MAX_TEAM_NAME_LEN
    team = create_team(name=max_name)
    team_id = team["id"]

    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/teams")

    row = page.locator(f"[data-testid='team-row-{team_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()

    row.locator("[data-testid='btn-delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete-btn']").click()

    expect(page.locator(f"[data-testid='team-row-{team_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    audit = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=team_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written for max-length team deletion"
    assert audit["entity_id"] == team_id

tests.name: test_delete_already_deleted_team_returns_400_or_404
preconditions: A team has already been deleted (state: deleted)
actions: Delete the team once via API (state: active -> deleted)
Attempt to delete the same team a second time via UI or API
Assert the system rejects the second deletion
expected: No duplicate audit record written for second attempt
trace: R-WEB-011: error pathway 400 on validation error
